error_signature: No binary data found in input. Check previous node output.
title: Binary data becomes undefined after remapping
symptom: After remapping the binary key from 'data' to 'audio', the next Code node reports that no binary data is found, leading to an error.
applies_when: Using Webhook node to receive binary data
Remapping binary key in Code node
not_this_if: Binary data is correctly referenced in subsequent nodes
Error occurs in a different node or context
root_cause: The remapping of the binary key from 'data' to 'audio' may not be correctly implemented, causing the subsequent node to fail in finding the expected binary data. This can happen if the return format is not properly structured or if the binary data is not correctly assigned.
fix_steps: Use the provided function to rename the binary key in the Code node: renameBinary($input.item.binary, 'data', 'audio');
Ensure the return statement in the Code node is structured as an object, not an array: return { json: $json, binary: { audio } };
Check the output of the previous node to confirm the binary data is present before the remapping.
agent_instruction: Implement the renaming function and ensure the return format is correct in the Code node.
